$comment: Full 9-field catalog for SQL injection (previously a null code-review catalog).

patterns.what_to_look_for: User input concatenated or interpolated directly into a SQL string instead of a parameterized query / bound parameter.
source_indicators: raw query API with request data
ORDER BY / column name from request
blackbox_indicators: boolean/time-based response differences on quote/operator injection
evidence_required: a benign payload that changes the query's logic or provokes a DB error
false_positive_checks: input is an integer cast before use
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Send a benign boolean/time probe to the entry point and confirm a query-logic change (no destructive payload).
patterns.reporting_guidance: Cite the sink file:line and the tainted param path; report severity by data sensitivity + auth context.

patterns.what_to_look_for: ORDER BY / column / table identifier taken from request input (parameterization does not cover identifiers).
source_indicators: sort/order param mapped into the query identifier position
blackbox_indicators: changing the sort param changes column ordering unexpectedly or errors
false_positive_checks: value mapped through a fixed allow-list before use
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Probe the sort/column param against a known allow-list bypass; confirm identifier injection.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Note that parameterized queries do NOT protect identifier positions — allow-list is the fix.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Second-order injection: stored input later concatenated into a query in a different code path.
source_indicators: value persisted then read into a raw query elsewhere
no encoding on the read path
blackbox_indicators: payload stored in one feature manifests in another's query
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Store a benign marker via the write path, observe it reach the query sink via the read path.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Trace the full store→read data flow; second-order issues are easily missed by per-endpoint review.

patterns.what_to_look_for: ORM 'raw' / 'where(string)' / fragment escape hatches fed request data.
blackbox_indicators: injection observable only on the raw-escape-hatch endpoints
false_positive_checks: bound parameters passed alongside the raw fragment
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Probe only the endpoints using the ORM raw escape hatch.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Call out that the ORM is safe by default but the raw escape hatch opts out.
